nodes.name: penicillin beta-lactam
nodes.def: "Penicilins (Penams) are a group of antibiotics derived from Penicillium fungi that share a skeleton beta-lactam moiety fused with a thiazolidine ring. Penicillin-like antibiotics are historically significant because they are the first drugs that were effective against many previously serious diseases such as syphilis and Staphylococcus infections. Penicillins are still widely used today, though many types of bacteria are now resistant. All penicillins are beta-lactam antibiotics in the penam sub-group, and are used in the treatment of bacterial infections caused by susceptible, usually Gram-positive, organisms." [PMID:3889939]
nodes.label: penicillin beta-lactam
nodes.title: ARO:3000008: penicillin beta-lactam; "Penicilins (Penams) are a group of antibiotics derived from Penicillium fungi that share a skeleton beta-lactam moiety fused with a thiazolidine ring. Penicillin-like antibiotics are historically significant because they are the first drugs that were effective against many previously serious diseases such as syphilis and Staphylococcus infections. Penicillins are still widely used today, though many types of bacteria are now resistant. All penicillins are beta-lactam antibiotics in the penam sub-group, and are used in the treatment of bacterial infections caused by susceptible, usually Gram-positive, organisms." [PMID:3889939]

nodes.name: triclosan
nodes.def: "Triclosan is a common antibacterial agent added to many consumer products as a biocide.  It is an inhibitor of fatty acid biosynthesis by blocking enoyl-carrier protein reductase (FabI)." [PMID:19768684, PMID:10196195]
nodes.label: triclosan
nodes.title: ARO:3000870: triclosan; "Triclosan is a common antibacterial agent added to many consumer products as a biocide.  It is an inhibitor of fatty acid biosynthesis by blocking enoyl-carrier protein reductase (FabI)." [PMID:19768684, PMID:10196195]

nodes.name: tetracycline
nodes.def: "Tetracycline is a broad-spectrum polyketide antibiotic produced by many Streptomyces. It works by inhibiting action of the prokaryotic 30S ribosome." [PMID:19862477, PMID:11381101]
nodes.label: tetracycline
nodes.title: ARO:0000051: tetracycline; "Tetracycline is a broad-spectrum polyketide antibiotic produced by many Streptomyces. It works by inhibiting action of the prokaryotic 30S ribosome." [PMID:19862477, PMID:11381101]

nodes.name: disinfecting agents and antiseptics
nodes.def: "Disinfectants that can also interact with antimicrobial resistance mechanisms, e.g. molecule efflux, and thus are the targets of disinfectant resistance." []
nodes.label: disinfecting agents and antiseptics
nodes.title: ARO:3005386: disinfecting agents and antiseptics; "Disinfectants that can also interact with antimicrobial resistance mechanisms, e.g. molecule efflux, and thus are the targets of disinfectant resistance." []

nodes.name: first-generation cephalosporin
nodes.def: "First-generation cephalosporin antibiotics include those with a relatively narrow spectrum of activity, primarily effective against gram-positive cocci and with minimal coverage against gram-negative bacteria." []
nodes.label: first-generation cephalosporin
nodes.title: ARO:3009105: first-generation cephalosporin; "First-generation cephalosporin antibiotics include those with a relatively narrow spectrum of activity, primarily effective against gram-positive cocci and with minimal coverage against gram-negative bacteria." []

nodes.name: AcrAB-TolC
nodes.def: "AcrAB-TolC is a tripartite RND efflux system that confers resistance to tetracycline, chloramphenicol, ampicillin, nalidixic acid, and rifampin  in Gram-negative bacteria. The system spans the cell membrane (AcrB) and the outer-membrane (TolC), and is linked together in the periplasm by AcrA." [PMID:8550435, PMID:21513882]
nodes.label: AcrAB-TolC
nodes.title: ARO:3000384: AcrAB-TolC; "AcrAB-TolC is a tripartite RND efflux system that confers resistance to tetracycline, chloramphenicol, ampicillin, nalidixic acid, and rifampin  in Gram-negative bacteria. The system spans the cell membrane (AcrB) and the outer-membrane (TolC), and is linked together in the periplasm by AcrA." [PMID:8550435, PMID:21513882]

nodes.name: penicillin with extended spectrum
nodes.def: "Pencillin-like antibiotics with extended spectrum activity gram-negative bacteria, such as ampicillin and similar antibiotic compounds." []
nodes.label: penicillin with extended spectrum
nodes.title: ARO:3009123: penicillin with extended spectrum; "Pencillin-like antibiotics with extended spectrum activity gram-negative bacteria, such as ampicillin and similar antibiotic compounds." []

nodes.name: reduced permeability to antibiotic
nodes.def: "Reduction in permeability to antibiotic, generally through reduced production of porins, can provide resistance." [PMID:2848006, PMID:19100346]
nodes.label: reduced permeability to antibiotic
nodes.title: ARO:3000244: reduced permeability to antibiotic; "Reduction in permeability to antibiotic, generally through reduced production of porins, can provide resistance." [PMID:2848006, PMID:19100346]

nodes.name: protein modulating permeability to antibiotic
nodes.def: "Enzymes or other proteins either directly or indirectly reducing overall permeability to antibiotics." [PMID:19100346, PMID:2848006]
nodes.label: protein modulating permeability to antibiotic
nodes.title: ARO:3000270: protein modulating permeability to antibiotic; "Enzymes or other proteins either directly or indirectly reducing overall permeability to antibiotics." [PMID:19100346, PMID:2848006]

nodes.name: rifampin
nodes.def: "Rifampin is a semi-synthetic rifamycin, and inhibits RNA synthesis by binding to RNA polymerase. Rifampin is the mainstay agent for the treatment of tuberculosis, leprosy and complicated Gram-positive infections." [PMID:15667909, PMID:6356275]
nodes.label: rifampin
nodes.title: ARO:3000169: rifampin; "Rifampin is a semi-synthetic rifamycin, and inhibits RNA synthesis by binding to RNA polymerase. Rifampin is the mainstay agent for the treatment of tuberculosis, leprosy and complicated Gram-positive infections." [PMID:15667909, PMID:6356275]

nodes.name: marA
nodes.def: "In the presence of antibiotic stress, E. coli overexpresses the global activator protein MarA, which besides inducing MDR efflux pump AcrAB, also down- regulates synthesis of the porin OmpF." [PMID:12027588, PMID:9333027, PMID:2848006]
nodes.label: marA
nodes.title: ARO:3000263: marA; "In the presence of antibiotic stress, E. coli overexpresses the global activator protein MarA, which besides inducing MDR efflux pump AcrAB, also down- regulates synthesis of the porin OmpF." [PMID:12027588, PMID:9333027, PMID:2848006]

nodes.name: chloramphenicol
nodes.def: "Chloramphenicol is a bacteriostatic antimicrobial originally derived from the bacterium Streptomyces venezuelae. It was the first antibiotic to be manufactured synthetically on a large scale. It functions by inhibiting peptidyl transferase activity of the bacterial ribosome, binding to A2451 and A2452 residues in the 23S rRNA of the 50S ribosomal subunit and preventing peptide bond formation." [PMID:17737966]
nodes.label: chloramphenicol
nodes.title: ARO:3000385: chloramphenicol; "Chloramphenicol is a bacteriostatic antimicrobial originally derived from the bacterium Streptomyces venezuelae. It was the first antibiotic to be manufactured synthetically on a large scale. It functions by inhibiting peptidyl transferase activity of the bacterial ribosome, binding to A2451 and A2452 residues in the 23S rRNA of the 50S ribosomal subunit and preventing peptide bond formation." [PMID:17737966]

nodes.name: carbapenem
nodes.def: "Carbapenems are a class of beta-lactam antibiotics with a broad spectrum of antibacterial activity, and have a structure which renders them highly resistant to beta-lactamases. Carbapenem antibiotics are bactericidal, and act by inhibiting the synthesis of the peptidoglycan layer of bacterial cell walls. The peptidoglycan layer is important for cell wall structural integrity, especially in Gram-positive organisms." [PMID:11585791, PMID:15673804]
nodes.label: carbapenem
nodes.title: ARO:0000020: carbapenem; "Carbapenems are a class of beta-lactam antibiotics with a broad spectrum of antibacterial activity, and have a structure which renders them highly resistant to beta-lactamases. Carbapenem antibiotics are bactericidal, and act by inhibiting the synthesis of the peptidoglycan layer of bacterial cell walls. The peptidoglycan layer is important for cell wall structural integrity, especially in Gram-positive organisms." [PMID:11585791, PMID:15673804]

nodes.name: glycylcycline
nodes.def: "Glycylcyclines are a new class of antibiotics derived from tetracycline. These tetracycline analogues are specifically designed to overcome two common mechanisms of tetracycline resistance. Presently, there is only one glycylcycline antibiotic for clinical use: tigecycline. It works by inhibiting action of the prokaryotic 30S ribosome, preventing the binding of aminoacyl-tRNA." [PMID:11381101]
nodes.label: glycylcycline
nodes.title: ARO:0000042: glycylcycline; "Glycylcyclines are a new class of antibiotics derived from tetracycline. These tetracycline analogues are specifically designed to overcome two common mechanisms of tetracycline resistance. Presently, there is only one glycylcycline antibiotic for clinical use: tigecycline. It works by inhibiting action of the prokaryotic 30S ribosome, preventing the binding of aminoacyl-tRNA." [PMID:11381101]

nodes.name: protein(s) and two-component regulatory system modulating antibiotic efflux
nodes.def: "Protein(s) and two component regulatory systems that directly or indirectly change rates of antibiotic efflux." [PMID:14572535, PMID:24878531]
nodes.label: protein(s) and two-component regulatory system modulating antibiotic efflux
nodes.title: ARO:3000451: protein(s) and two-component regulatory system modulating antibiotic efflux; "Protein(s) and two component regulatory systems that directly or indirectly change rates of antibiotic efflux." [PMID:14572535, PMID:24878531]

nodes.name: rifamycin antibiotic
nodes.def: "Rifamycin antibiotics are a group of broad-spectrum ansamycin antibiotics that inhibit bacterial RNA polymerase by binding to a highly conserved region, blocking the oligonucleotide exit tunnel, and preventing the extension of nascent mRNAs." [PMID:15700959]
nodes.label: rifamycin antibiotic
nodes.title: ARO:3000157: rifamycin antibiotic; "Rifamycin antibiotics are a group of broad-spectrum ansamycin antibiotics that inhibit bacterial RNA polymerase by binding to a highly conserved region, blocking the oligonucleotide exit tunnel, and preventing the extension of nascent mRNAs." [PMID:15700959]

nodes.name: General Bacterial Porin with reduced permeability to beta-lactams
nodes.def: "These are GBPs that are associated with decreased susceptibility to beta-lactams either through mutations in the porin protein, absence of the porin protein, or expression of the porin protein." []
nodes.label: General Bacterial Porin with reduced permeability to beta-lactams
nodes.title: ARO:3004281: General Bacterial Porin with reduced permeability to beta-lactams; "These are GBPs that are associated with decreased susceptibility to beta-lactams either through mutations in the porin protein, absence of the porin protein, or expression of the porin protein." []

nodes.name: porin OmpF
nodes.def: "In the presence of antibiotic stress, E. coli overexpresses the global activator protein MarA, which besides inducing MDR efflux pump AcrAB, also down- regulates synthesis of the porin OmpF." [PMID:2848006, PMID:19100346]
nodes.label: porin OmpF
nodes.title: ARO:3000265: porin OmpF; "In the presence of antibiotic stress, E. coli overexpresses the global activator protein MarA, which besides inducing MDR efflux pump AcrAB, also down- regulates synthesis of the porin OmpF." [PMID:2848006, PMID:19100346]

nodes.name: cefalotin
nodes.def: "Cefalotin is a semisynthetic cephalosporin antibiotic activate against staphylococci. It is resistant to staphylococci beta-lactamases but hydrolyzed by enterobacterial beta-lactamases." [PMID:14251901, PMID:7571387]
nodes.label: cefalotin
nodes.title: ARO:3000704: cefalotin; "Cefalotin is a semisynthetic cephalosporin antibiotic activate against staphylococci. It is resistant to staphylococci beta-lactamases but hydrolyzed by enterobacterial beta-lactamases." [PMID:14251901, PMID:7571387]

nodes.name: antibiotic efflux
nodes.def: "Antibiotic resistance via the transport of antibiotics out of the cell." [PMID:19678712]
nodes.label: antibiotic efflux
nodes.title: ARO:0010000: antibiotic efflux; "Antibiotic resistance via the transport of antibiotics out of the cell." [PMID:19678712]

nodes.name: monobactam
nodes.def: "Monobactams are a class of beta-lactam antibiotics with a broad spectrum of antibacterial activity, and have a structure which renders them highly resistant to beta-lactamases. Unlike penams and cephems, monobactams do not have any ring fused to its four-member lactam structure. Monobactam antibiotics are bactericidal, and act by inhibiting the synthesis of the peptidoglycan layer of bacterial cell walls. The peptidoglycan layer is important for cell wall structural integrity, especially in Gram-positive organisms." [PMID:3871589, PMID:15673804, PMID:02669628, PMID:11585791]
nodes.label: monobactam
nodes.title: ARO:0000004: monobactam; "Monobactams are a class of beta-lactam antibiotics with a broad spectrum of antibacterial activity, and have a structure which renders them highly resistant to beta-lactamases. Unlike penams and cephems, monobactams do not have any ring fused to its four-member lactam structure. Monobactam antibiotics are bactericidal, and act by inhibiting the synthesis of the peptidoglycan layer of bacterial cell walls. The peptidoglycan layer is important for cell wall structural integrity, especially in Gram-positive organisms." [PMID:3871589, PMID:15673804, PMID:02669628, PMID:11585791]

nodes.name: ampicillin
nodes.def: "Ampicillin is a penicillin derivative that is highly acid stable, with its activity similar to benzylpenicillin." [PMID:44765, PMID:3224398]
nodes.label: ampicillin
nodes.title: ARO:3000637: ampicillin; "Ampicillin is a penicillin derivative that is highly acid stable, with its activity similar to benzylpenicillin." [PMID:44765, PMID:3224398]

nodes.name: General Bacterial Porin (GBP)
nodes.def: "General bacterial porins are a family of porins found in the outer membrane of Gram-negative bacteria. They catalyze the energy-independent transport of small molecules across the outer membranes of bacteria. Most of the porins of these family are homotrimeric and have variable selectivity, ranging from being cation-selective, anion-selective or selective for different compounds. The most well characterized members of this family are the OmpF and OmpC porins of Escherichia coli. This family is also known as the classical porins." [PMID:18997824]
nodes.label: General Bacterial Porin (GBP)
nodes.title: ARO:3004277: General Bacterial Porin (GBP); "General bacterial porins are a family of porins found in the outer membrane of Gram-negative bacteria. They catalyze the energy-independent transport of small molecules across the outer membranes of bacteria. Most of the porins of these family are homotrimeric and have variable selectivity, ranging from being cation-selective, anion-selective or selective for different compounds. The most well characterized members of this family are the OmpF and OmpC porins of Escherichia coli. This family is also known as the classical porins." [PMID:18997824]

nodes.name: efflux pump complex or subunit conferring antibiotic resistance
nodes.def: "Efflux proteins that pump antibiotic out of a cell to confer resistance." []
nodes.label: efflux pump complex or subunit conferring antibiotic resistance
nodes.title: ARO:3000159: efflux pump complex or subunit conferring antibiotic resistance; "Efflux proteins that pump antibiotic out of a cell to confer resistance." []

nodes.name: resistance-nodulation-cell division (RND) antibiotic efflux pump
nodes.def: "Directed pumping of antibiotic out of a cell to confer resistance. Resistance-nodulation-division (RND) proteins are found in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells and have diverse substrate specificities and physiological roles. However, there are relatively few RND transporters and they are secondary transporters, energized not by ATP binding/hydrolysis but by proton movement down the transmembrane electrochemical gradient." [PMID:15582398, PMID:16915237, PMID:19664953]
nodes.label: resistance-nodulation-cell division (RND) antibiotic efflux pump
nodes.title: ARO:0010004: resistance-nodulation-cell division (RND) antibiotic efflux pump; "Directed pumping of antibiotic out of a cell to confer resistance. Resistance-nodulation-division (RND) proteins are found in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells and have diverse substrate specificities and physiological roles. However, there are relatively few RND transporters and they are secondary transporters, energized not by ATP binding/hydrolysis but by proton movement down the transmembrane electrochemical gradient." [PMID:15582398, PMID:16915237, PMID:19664953]

nodes.name: cephalosporin
nodes.def: "Cephalosporins are a class of beta-lactam antibiotics, containing the beta-lactam ring fused with a dihydrothiazolidine ring. Together with cephamycins they belong to a sub-group called cephems. Cephalosporin are bactericidal, and act by inhibiting the synthesis of the peptidoglycan layer of bacterial cell walls. The peptidoglycan layer is important for cell wall structural integrity, especially in Gram-positive organisms." [PMID:15673804, PMID:11585791, PMID:6810737]
nodes.label: cephalosporin
nodes.title: ARO:0000032: cephalosporin; "Cephalosporins are a class of beta-lactam antibiotics, containing the beta-lactam ring fused with a dihydrothiazolidine ring. Together with cephamycins they belong to a sub-group called cephems. Cephalosporin are bactericidal, and act by inhibiting the synthesis of the peptidoglycan layer of bacterial cell walls. The peptidoglycan layer is important for cell wall structural integrity, especially in Gram-positive organisms." [PMID:15673804, PMID:11585791, PMID:6810737]

nodes.name: beta-lactam antibiotic
nodes.def: "Beta-lactam antibiotics are a broad class of antibiotics that include penams (penicillin derivatives), cephems (cephalosporins), monobactams, and carbapenems. These antibiotic agents contain a beta-lactam nucleus in its molecular structure. They are the most widely-used group of antibiotics." [PMID:3889939]
nodes.label: beta-lactam antibiotic
nodes.title: ARO:3000007: beta-lactam antibiotic; "Beta-lactam antibiotics are a broad class of antibiotics that include penams (penicillin derivatives), cephems (cephalosporins), monobactams, and carbapenems. These antibiotic agents contain a beta-lactam nucleus in its molecular structure. They are the most widely-used group of antibiotics." [PMID:3889939]

nodes.name: fluoroquinolone antibiotic
nodes.def: "The fluoroquinolones are a family of synthetic broad-spectrum antibiotics that are 4-quinolone-3-carboxylates. These compounds interact with topoisomerase II (DNA gyrase) to disrupt bacterial DNA replication, damage DNA, and cause cell death." [PMID:3802748, PMID:3311572, PMID:23010009]
nodes.label: fluoroquinolone antibiotic
nodes.title: ARO:0000001: fluoroquinolone antibiotic; "The fluoroquinolones are a family of synthetic broad-spectrum antibiotics that are 4-quinolone-3-carboxylates. These compounds interact with topoisomerase II (DNA gyrase) to disrupt bacterial DNA replication, damage DNA, and cause cell death." [PMID:3802748, PMID:3311572, PMID:23010009]

nodes.name: tigecycline
nodes.def: "Tigecycline is an glycylcycline antibiotic. It works by inhibiting action of the prokaryotic 30S ribosome." [PMID:11381101, PMID:19862477]
nodes.label: tigecycline
nodes.title: ARO:0000030: tigecycline; "Tigecycline is an glycylcycline antibiotic. It works by inhibiting action of the prokaryotic 30S ribosome." [PMID:11381101, PMID:19862477]

nodes.name: tetracycline antibiotic
nodes.def: "These antibiotics are derived from tetracycline, a polyketide antibiotic that inhibits the 30S subunit of bacterial ribosomes." [PMID:11381101]
nodes.label: tetracycline antibiotic
nodes.title: ARO:3000050: tetracycline antibiotic; "These antibiotics are derived from tetracycline, a polyketide antibiotic that inhibits the 30S subunit of bacterial ribosomes." [PMID:11381101]

nodes.name: phenicol antibiotic
nodes.def: "Phenicols are broad spectrum bacteriostatic antibiotics acting on bacterial protein synthesis. More specifically, the phenicols block peptide elongation by binding to the peptidyltansferase centre of the 70S ribosome." [PMID:12860128]
nodes.label: phenicol antibiotic
nodes.title: ARO:3000387: phenicol antibiotic; "Phenicols are broad spectrum bacteriostatic antibiotics acting on bacterial protein synthesis. More specifically, the phenicols block peptide elongation by binding to the peptidyltansferase centre of the 70S ribosome." [PMID:12860128]
